name: compromised-vendor
description: A trusted external vendor's account (a Guest in your tenant) is compromised. The attacker uses the existing share permissions to access internal files instead of needing fresh credentials. Trigger: Detect-MassExternalShare on a Guest user or Detect-AnomalousLocationSignIn for a Guest.
trigger: Guest user activity from a country the vendor is not based in, or sudden bulk-access to files outside the vendor's normal project scope.
expectedFindings: Guest user accessing sites / files outside their normal project
External shares created during the incident window
Sign-in from outside the vendor organization's known geo
recoveryNotes: Notify the vendor immediately (they may not know they are compromised). Consider revoking the Guest user entirely rather than just blocking sign-in -- you can always re-invite when the vendor confirms recovery. Audit shared files for unauthorized access in the same audit window.
